checks.a_analysis: The output document does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. The text present in the metadata title ('20190416 Nc 911 Board Minutes Approved 687248C4') is a known category of allowed pipeline-generated content for accessibility/metadata and is not considered a fabrication defect for the purpose of content fidelity audit.

All contact information found in the output, specifically the conference bridge number '888-808-6929' and ID '8153047#', accurately reflects the information in the source document. No fabricated phone numbers or URLs were detected.

Both pages of the source PDF are represented in the structured output. While there are significant omissions of content within sections (evaluated under C5), no entire page or high-level section heading was skipped.

The primary table on page 1 (Tabs/Topics/Presenters) is correctly represented with the same number of data rows as the source. Other items that appear as columns in the source (items 6-12) are reformatted as lists and paragraphs, which is an acceptable structural change.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output document suffers from significant text omissions across several critical sections. Specifically: 1) The financial details (Fund Balance and Disbursement figures) for items 4b (NG 911 Fund), 4c (CMRS Fund), 4d (PSAP Fund), and 4e (Grant Fund) are entirely missing. 2) The names of the peer reviewers under item 4g (i-v) are missing. 3) The detailed sub-items for the Executive Director Report (item 5, sub-items a-f) are entirely absent. These omissions collectively represent a loss of over 25% of the descriptive content in their respective sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Financial figures for NG 911, CMRS, PSAP, and Grant Funds (e.g., 'November 2018 Fund Balance $27,867,858'); Sub-items for 4g (i. Vicki Callicutt... v. Tracy Trogdon); Sub-items for 5 (a) New Board Member Orientation... f) Reminder of Nominations...)
checks.b_evidence_output: [The content is entirely absent from the corresponding sections in the JSON]

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Beyond the significant omissions identified in C5, there are no wording changes that alter the meaning of the preserved text. The mapping of presenters to agenda topics is maintained correctly.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output significantly alters the visual organization of the document in two ways. First, the detailed financial data associated with sub-items 4b through 4e (e.g., 'November 2018 Fund Balance $27,867,858') is entirely missing from the bulleted list in the HTML rendering. Second, the two-column relationship between 'Topic' and 'Presenter' for items 6 through 13 is destroyed. In the source, these are aligned horizontally; in the HTML, all topics are listed first, followed by a separate block of presenter names, making it impossible to visually associate which person is presenting which topic.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 1, Item 4b: 'NG 911 Fund November 2018 Fund Balance $27,867,858...'. PDF Page 2: '6. Education Committee Report Mike Reitz'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML bulleted list for Item 4 only contains 'b) NG 911 Fund' without any associated figures. Below the image, the HTML lists '6. Education Committee Report' through '13. Other', followed by a completely separate block of names starting with 'Mike Reitz'.

checks.a_analysis: The second half of the agenda (items 6-13) is visually presented as a two-column table/grid in the source PDF, pairing topics with presenters. In the HTML output, this structure is broken. The content is rendered as two distinct vertical lists: a list of committee reports/topics followed by a list of names. This renders the 'table' data incomprehensible as the row-level association is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source PDF Page 2 shows a tabular layout for items 6-13 with 'Topic' on the left and 'Presenter' on the right (e.g., 7. Funding Committee Report | David Bone).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the topics listed vertically, ending with '13. Other', followed by a separate block of text containing the names 'Mike Reitz', 'David Bone', etc.

checks.a_analysis: The primary meaningful image from the source PDF, the 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo, is correctly captured and rendered in the HTML output.

No body content is duplicated. While titles and headings like 'AGENDA' and 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING' appear in both the navigation sidebar (Table of Contents) and the main body, this is a standard and expected accessibility feature for HTML conversion.
